description:
You are a QA engineer preparing a test environment inventory.  
  A plain-text list of target environments has already been placed at  
  /home/user/env_setup/targets.txt  

  1. Review /home/user/env_setup/targets.txt.  
     • Every line contains exactly one environment name (letters only, all lowercase).  
     • There are no empty lines.  

  2. Produce a frequency report that counts how many times each environment appears.  
     • Create that report at the exact path /home/user/env_setup/targets_frequency.txt.  
     • Sort the report alphabetically by the environment name.  
     • Use the precise format:  
       <environment_name><single_space><count><newline>  
       Example (do NOT hard-code):  
       alpha 7⏎  
       beta 3⏎  
       gamma 1⏎  

     The file must end with a single trailing newline, no extra blank lines or spaces.

  3. Confirm by displaying the contents of /home/user/env_setup/targets_frequency.txt to the terminal.

  Hints (do NOT reveal exact commands):  
  • Standard GNU text utilities (sort, uniq, cat, etc.) are available.  
  • Think in terms of a small pipeline (≈3-4 commands) to transform and save the data.  

  The automated test will:  
  • Verify that /home/user/env_setup/targets_frequency.txt exists.  
  • Check that its contents follow the required “name count” pattern, alphabetically sorted, with a single trailing newline and no additional output.
truth:
Initial file provided before the task starts:
  Path: /home/user/env_setup/targets.txt  
  Exact contents (↵ represents a newline):

    dev↵
    qa↵
    staging↵
    qa↵
    dev↵
    prod↵
    qa↵

  Expected file created by the agent:
  Path: /home/user/env_setup/targets_frequency.txt  
  Required contents (↵ represents the single trailing newline):

    dev 2↵
    prod 1↵
    qa 3↵
    staging 1↵
name: task_000000_815fce26
